revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 画像=| 名称=高田神社| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669| 祭神=[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]| 社格=式内社 郷...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 画像=| 名称=高田神社| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669| 祭神=[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]| 社格=式内社 郷社| 例祭=4月28日}}
'''高田神社'''（たかたじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]（旧[[吉城郡]][[古川町 (岐阜県)|古川町]]）にある[[神社]]。
[[飛騨国]][[吉城郡|荒城郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式名社]]の高田神社の論社である（別の論社は[[貴船神社 (飛騨市)|貴船神社]]）。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。一説では[[敏達天皇]]6年（[[577年]]）、飛騨国に「日奉部」が置かれさい、その[[伴造]]が高魂命を祀ったのが最初という。
[[建武の新政]]に功績のあった[[姉小路家綱]]は、[[建武]]元年（[[1334年]]）に飛騨[[国司]]に任命される。家綱はこの高田神社を崇敬し、隣接して館を設け飛騨の政治の中枢となる。しかし、[[姉小路氏]]が[[金森長近]]により滅亡すると衰退する。
[[江戸時代]]は「白山社」「白山権現宮」に改称される。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、高田神社に改称する。しかし、正式に式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。
明治40年（[[1907年]]）、諏訪神社、多度神社、加茂神社を合祀する。
== 祭神 == *主神 :[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]] *合祀 :[[菊理媛神|白山比咩命]] :[[建御名方神|建御名方命]] :[[賀茂別雷命]] :[[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]] [[飛騨総社]]
== 例祭 == 見所は神楽獅子、[[火縄銃]]発砲、神輿行列である。
*神楽獅子 **二人一組による雌雄二組の[[獅子舞]]で、演目6種ある。岐阜県の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。例祭では数回行なわれる。 *火縄銃発砲 **例祭の要所要所で行なわれ、神域を清める。火縄銃は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、姉小路時光（小島時光）が奉納したものと伝えられる。 *神輿行列 **[[サカキ|榊]]、[[天狗]]を先頭に、[[鎧]]武者、鶏闘楽、雌雄獅子、太鼓、笛、[[巫女]]、神輿と続く総勢数百名の行列。
== 所在地 == *岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669
== 交通機関 == *[[高山本線]][[杉崎駅]]より約2km
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:郷社|たかたしんしや]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社|たかたしんしや]] [[Category:飛騨市|たかたしんしや]]

{{神社| 画像=| 名称=高田神社| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669| 祭神=[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]| 社格=式内社 郷社| 例祭=4月28日}}

== 所在地 == *岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669
== 交通機関 == *[[高山本線]][[杉崎駅]]より約2km

'''高田神社'''（たかたじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]（旧[[吉城郡]][[古川町]]）にある[[神社]]。
[[飛騨国]][[吉城郡|荒城郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式名社]]の高田神社の論社である（別の論社は[[貴船神社 (飛騨市)|貴船神社]]）。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。一説では[[敏達天皇]]6年（[[577年]]）、飛騨国に「日奉部」が置かれさい、その[[伴造]]が高魂命を祀ったのが最初という。
[[建武の新政]]に功績のあった[[姉小路家綱]]は、[[建武 (日本)|建武]]元年（[[1334年]]）に飛騨[[国司]]に任命される。家綱はこの高田神社を崇敬し、隣接して館を設け飛騨の政治の中枢となる。しかし、[[姉小路氏]]が[[金森長近]]により滅亡すると衰退する。
[[江戸時代]]は「白山社」「白山権現宮」に改称される。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、高田神社に改称する。しかし、正式に式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。
明治40年（[[1907年]]）、諏訪神社、多度神社、加茂神社を合祀する。
== 祭神 == * 主神 : [[タカミムスビ|高魂命]] * 合祀 : [[菊理媛神|白山比咩命]] : [[建御名方神|建御名方命]] : [[賀茂別雷命]] : [[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]]
== 例祭 == 見所は神楽獅子、[[火縄銃]]発砲、神輿行列である。
* 神楽獅子 ** 二人一組による雌雄二組の[[獅子舞]]で、演目6種ある。岐阜県の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている。例祭では数回行なわれる。 * 火縄銃発砲 ** 例祭の要所要所で行なわれ、神域を清める。火縄銃は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、姉小路時光（小島時光）が奉納したものと伝えられる。 * 神輿行列 ** [[サカキ|榊]]、[[天狗]]を先頭に、[[鎧]]武者、鶏闘楽、雌雄獅子、太鼓、笛、[[巫女]]、神輿と続く総勢数百名の行列。
== 所在地 == * 岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669
== 交通機関 == * [[高山本線]][[杉崎駅]]より約2km

== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。一説では[[敏達天皇]]6年（[[577年]]）、飛騨国に「日奉部」が置かれさい、その[[伴造]]が高魂命を祀ったのが最初という。

[[江戸時代]]は「白山社」「白山権現宮」に改称される。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、高田神社に改称する。しかし、正式に式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。

* 神楽獅子 ** 二人一組による雌雄二組の[[獅子舞]]で、演目6種ある。岐阜県の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=高田神社の神楽獅子 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref>。例祭では数回行なわれる。 * 火縄銃発砲 ** 例祭の要所要所で行なわれ、神域を清める。火縄銃は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、姉小路時光（小島時光）が奉納したものと伝えられる。 * 神輿行列 ** [[サカキ|榊]]、[[天狗]]を先頭に、[[鎧]]武者、鶏闘楽、雌雄獅子、太鼓、笛、[[巫女]]、神輿と続く総勢数百名の行列。
== 文化財 == ;県指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 高田神社の神楽獅子<ref name="kagurajishi"/> ;市指定有形文化財 * 高田神社のかぶと<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=古川町文化財案内 |format=PDF |publisher=飛騨市 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * 高田神社随神像<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/> ;市指定天然記念物 * 高田神社の大五本スギ<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/>
== 所在地 == * 岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669
== 交通機関 == * [[高山本線]][[杉崎駅]]より約2km

{{神社| 画像=| 名称=高田神社| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}}| 祭神=[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]| 社格=式内社 郷社| 例祭=4月28日}}
'''高田神社'''（たかたじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]（旧[[吉城郡]][[古川町]]）にある[[神社]]。
[[飛騨国]][[荒城郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式名社]]の高田神社の[[論社]]である（別の論社は[[貴船神社 (飛騨市)|貴船神社]]）。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。一説では[[敏達天皇]]6年（[[577年]]）、飛騨国に「日奉部」が置かれさい、その[[伴造]]が高魂命を祀ったのが最初という。
[[建武の新政]]に功績のあった[[姉小路家綱]]は、[[建武 (日本)|建武]]元年（[[1334年]]）に飛騨[[国司]]に任命される。家綱はこの高田神社を崇敬し、隣接して館を設け飛騨の政治の中枢となる。しかし、[[姉小路氏]]が[[金森長近]]により滅亡すると衰退する。
[[江戸時代]]は「白山社」「白山権現宮」に改称される。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、高田神社に改称する。しかし、正式に式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。
明治40年（[[1907年]]）、諏訪神社、多度神社、加茂神社を合祀する。
== 祭神 == * 主神 : [[タカミムスビ|高魂命]] * 合祀 : [[菊理媛神|白山比咩命]] : [[建御名方神|建御名方命]] : [[賀茂別雷命]] : [[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]]
== 例祭 == 見所は神楽獅子、[[火縄銃]]発砲、神輿行列である。
* 神楽獅子 ** 二人一組による雌雄二組の[[獅子舞]]で、演目6種ある。岐阜県の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=高田神社の神楽獅子 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref>。例祭では数回行なわれる。 * 火縄銃発砲 ** 例祭の要所要所で行なわれ、神域を清める。火縄銃は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、姉小路時光（小島時光）が奉納したものと伝えられる。 * 神輿行列 ** [[サカキ|榊]]、[[天狗]]を先頭に、[[鎧]]武者、鶏闘楽、雌雄獅子、太鼓、笛、[[巫女]]、神輿と続く総勢数百名の行列。
== 文化財 == ;県指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 高田神社の神楽獅子<ref name="kagurajishi"/> ;市指定有形文化財 * 高田神社のかぶと<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=古川町文化財案内 |format=PDF |publisher=飛騨市 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * 高田神社随神像<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/> ;市指定天然記念物 * 高田神社の大五本スギ<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/>
== 所在地 == * 岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669
== 交通機関 == * [[高山本線]][[杉崎駅]]より約2km

{{神社| 名称=高田神社| 画像=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]]| 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}}| 祭神=[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]| 社格=式内社 郷社| 例祭=4月28日}}
'''高田神社'''（たかたじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]（旧[[吉城郡]][[古川町]]）にある[[神社]]。
[[飛騨国]][[荒城郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式名社]]の高田神社の[[論社]]である（別の論社は[[貴船神社 (飛騨市)|貴船神社]]）。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。一説では[[敏達天皇]]6年（[[577年]]）、飛騨国に「日奉部」が置かれさい、その[[伴造]]が高魂命を祀ったのが最初という。
[[建武の新政]]に功績のあった[[姉小路家綱]]は、[[建武 (日本)|建武]]元年（[[1334年]]）に飛騨[[国司]]に任命される。家綱はこの高田神社を崇敬し、隣接して館を設け飛騨の政治の中枢となる。しかし、[[姉小路氏]]が[[金森長近]]により滅亡すると衰退する。
[[江戸時代]]は「白山社」「白山権現宮」に改称される。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、高田神社に改称する。しかし、正式に式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。
明治40年（[[1907年]]）、諏訪神社、多度神社、加茂神社を合祀する。
== 祭神 == * 主神 : [[タカミムスビ|高魂命]] * 合祀 : [[菊理媛神|白山比咩命]] : [[建御名方神|建御名方命]] : [[賀茂別雷命]] : [[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]]
== 例祭 == 見所は神楽獅子、[[火縄銃]]発砲、神輿行列である。
* 神楽獅子 ** 二人一組による雌雄二組の[[獅子舞]]で、演目6種ある。岐阜県の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=高田神社の神楽獅子 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref>。例祭では数回行なわれる。 * 火縄銃発砲 ** 例祭の要所要所で行なわれ、神域を清める。火縄銃は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、姉小路時光（小島時光）が奉納したものと伝えられる。 * 神輿行列 ** [[サカキ|榊]]、[[天狗]]を先頭に、[[鎧]]武者、鶏闘楽、雌雄獅子、太鼓、笛、[[巫女]]、神輿と続く総勢数百名の行列。
== 文化財 == ;県指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 高田神社の神楽獅子<ref name="kagurajishi"/> ;市指定有形文化財 * 高田神社のかぶと<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=古川町文化財案内 |format=PDF |publisher=飛騨市 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * 高田神社随神像<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/> ;市指定天然記念物 * 高田神社の大五本スギ<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/>
== 交通アクセス == * [[高山本線]][[杉崎駅]]より約2km
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかたしんしや}} [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:飛騨市の歴史]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:栃木県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]]

== 例祭 == 見所は神楽獅子、[[火縄銃]]発砲、神輿行列である。
* 神楽獅子 ** 二人一組による雌雄二組の[[獅子舞]]で、演目6種ある。岐阜県の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=高田神社の神楽獅子 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref>。例祭では数回行なわれる。 * 火縄銃発砲 ** 例祭の要所要所で行なわれ、神域を清める。火縄銃は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、姉小路時光（小島時光）が奉納したものと伝えられる。 * 神輿行列 ** [[サカキ|榊]]、[[天狗]]を先頭に、[[鎧]]武者、鶏闘楽、雌雄獅子、太鼓、笛、[[巫女]]、神輿と続く総勢数百名の行列。
== 文化財 == ;県指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 高田神社の神楽獅子<ref name="kagurajishi"/> ;市指定有形文化財 * 高田神社のかぶと<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=古川町文化財案内 |format=PDF |publisher=飛騨市 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * 高田神社随神像<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/> ;市指定天然記念物 * 高田神社の大五本スギ<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/>
== 交通アクセス == * [[高山本線]][[杉崎駅]]より約2km
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかたしんしや}} [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:飛騨市の歴史]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:栃木県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:飛騨市の建築物]]

{{神社 | 名称=高田神社 | 画像=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | 所在地=岐阜県飛騨市古川町太江神垣内2669 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神=[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]] | 社格=式内社 郷社 [[金幣社]] | 例祭=4月28日 }}
'''高田神社'''（たかたじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[飛騨市]]（旧[[吉城郡]][[古川町]]）にある[[神社]]。
[[飛騨国]][[荒城郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式名社]]の高田神社の[[論社]]である（別の論社は[[貴船神社 (飛騨市)|貴船神社]]）。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。一説では[[敏達天皇]]6年（[[577年]]）、飛騨国に「[[日奉部]]」が置かれさい、その[[伴造]]が高魂命を祀ったのが最初という。
[[建武の新政]]に功績のあった[[姉小路家綱]]は、[[建武 (日本)|建武]]元年（[[1334年]]）に飛騨[[国司]]に任命される。家綱はこの高田神社を崇敬し、隣接して館を設け飛騨の政治の中枢となる。しかし、[[姉小路氏]]が[[金森長近]]により滅亡すると衰退する。
[[江戸時代]]は「白山社」「白山権現宮」に改称される。[[文化 (元号)|文化]]年間に[[吉田家]]により白山社が式内社高田神社であることが確認され、高田神社に改称する。しかし、正式に式内社高田神社と称することが認められたのは[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）のことである。
明治40年（[[1907年]]）、諏訪神社、多度神社、加茂神社を合祀する。
== 祭神 == * 主神 : [[タカミムスビ|高魂命]] * 合祀 : [[菊理媛神|白山比咩命]] : [[建御名方神|建御名方命]] : [[賀茂別雷命]] : [[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]]
== 例祭 == 見所は神楽獅子、[[火縄銃]]発砲、神輿行列である。
* 神楽獅子 ** 二人一組による雌雄二組の[[獅子舞]]で、演目6種ある。岐阜県の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=高田神社の神楽獅子 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref>。例祭では数回行なわれる。 * 火縄銃発砲 ** 例祭の要所要所で行なわれ、神域を清める。火縄銃は[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、姉小路時光（小島時光）が奉納したものと伝えられる。 * 神輿行列 ** [[サカキ|榊]]、[[天狗]]を先頭に、[[鎧]]武者、鶏闘楽、雌雄獅子、太鼓、笛、[[巫女]]、神輿と続く総勢数百名の行列。
== 文化財 == ;県指定重要無形民俗文化財 * 高田神社の神楽獅子<ref name="kagurajishi"/> ;市指定有形文化財 * 高田神社のかぶと<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=古川町文化財案内 |format=PDF |publisher=飛騨市 |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * 高田神社随神像<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/> ;市指定天然記念物 * 高田神社の大五本スギ<ref name="furukawacho bunkazai"/>
== 交通アクセス == * [[高山本線]][[杉崎駅]]より約2km
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]

revision.comment: Created page with " {{Shrine | Name=Takata Shrine | Image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | Location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | Coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | Deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | Festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]..."
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name=Takata Shrine | Image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | Location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | Coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | Deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | Festivals=April 28 }}
'''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Yoshida Clan (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * **Main Deity** * [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * **Enshrined Deities** * [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* **Kagura Lion Dance** * A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * **Matchlock Gun Firings** * Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * **Portable Shrine Procession** * A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

[[ja:高田神社_(飛騨市)]]

The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * **Portable Shrine Procession** * A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.

== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name=Takata Shrine | Image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | Location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | Coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | Deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | Festivals=April 28 }}
'''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Yoshida Clan (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * **Main Deity** * [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * **Enshrined Deities** * [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* **Kagura Lion Dance** * A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * **Matchlock Gun Firings** * Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * **Portable Shrine Procession** * A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Yoshida Clan (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. == Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* **Kagura Lion Dance** * A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * **Matchlock Gun Firings** * Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * **Portable Shrine Procession** * A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * **Portable Shrine Procession** * A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }}
'''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Yoshida Clan (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * **Main Deity** * [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * **Enshrined Deities** * [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] * [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* **Kagura Lion Dance** * A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * **Matchlock Gun Firings** * Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * **Portable Shrine Procession** * A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }}
'''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Yoshida Clan (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * '''Main Deity''' ''' [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * '''Enshrined Deities''' ''' [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ''' [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ''' [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ''' [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* '''Kagura Lion Dance''' ''' A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * '''Matchlock Gun Firings''' ''' Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * '''Portable Shrine Procession''' ''' A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * '''Matchlock Gun Firings''' ''' Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * '''Portable Shrine Procession''' ''' A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.

{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }}
'''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Yoshida Clan (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * '''Main deity''' ''' [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * '''Enshrined Deities''' ''' [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ''' [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ''' [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ''' [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* '''Kagura Lion Dance''' ''' A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * '''Matchlock Gun Firings''' ''' Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * '''Portable Shrine Procession''' ''' A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }}
'''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Yoshida Clan (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * '''Main deity''' ''' [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * '''Enshrined Deities''' ''' [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ''' [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ''' [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ''' [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* '''Kagura Lion Dance''' ''' A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * '''Matchlock Gun Firings''' ''' Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * '''Portable Shrine Procession''' ''' A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }}
**Takata Shrine** (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Yoshida Clan (Shinto)|ja|吉田家}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * **Main deity** ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * **Enshrined Deities** ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* **Kagura Lion Dance** ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * **Matchlock Gun Firings** ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * **Portable Shrine Procession** ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== Annual Festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: fixing parameters en masse
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{Shrine | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was appointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
