During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * A11roximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * A11roximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)|12=draft}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan|12=draft}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=draft}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗|12=draft}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女|12=draft}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.

It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=draft}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗|12=draft}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女|12=draft}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * A11roximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅|12=draft}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * A11roximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Kifune Shrine (飛騨市)" to "Kifune Shrine (Hida City)"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|lt=Yoshida clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|ja|巫女|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * A11roximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] {{ill|Sugisaki Station|ja|杉崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * A11roximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高田神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11672195]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|qid=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[:en:Yoshiki District|Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[:en:Anekōji clan|Anekōji clan]] to [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[:en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高田神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11672195]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[:en:Yoshiki District|Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[:en:Anekōji clan|Anekōji clan]] to [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[:en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高田神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11672195]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[:en:Yoshiki District|Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[:en:Anekōji clan|Anekōji clan]] to [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[:en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36|15|24.19|N|137|10|21.88|E|scale:20000}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|qq=}} | festivals=April 28 }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[Anekōji clan]] to [[Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[Kamo Wakeikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[sakaki]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * A11roximately 2 km from [[Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[:en:Anekōji clan|Anekōji clan]] to [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[:en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高田神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11672195]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[:en:Yoshiki District|Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[:en:Anekōji clan|Anekōji clan]] to [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[:en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[:en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高田神社 (飛騨市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11672195]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hida City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], formerly part of [[:en:Yoshiki District|Yoshiki District]] in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the [[:en:Anekōji clan|Anekōji clan]] to [[:en:Kanamori Nagachika|Kanamori Nagachika]], the shrine's prominence declined.
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the [[:en:Bunka|Bunka era]] (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** [[:en:Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto]] ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]].<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Cleyera japonica]], [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]], armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, [[:en:Miko|Miko]], and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]] [[:en:Sugisaki Station|Sugisaki Station]].
