One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />

He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, formerly part of {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District}} in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station}}.

'''Takata Shrine (Hida) (Q11672195)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hida Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! {{wikidata link|Q11672195}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == <!--interwikis from wikidata-->
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, formerly part of {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District}} in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, formerly part of {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District}} in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, formerly part of {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District}} in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, formerly part of {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District}} in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />

{{wikidata link|Q11672195}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == <!--interwikis from wikidata-->
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319|1=Hida, Gifu}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, formerly part of {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District|1=Yoshiki District}} in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan|1=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika|1=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica|1=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu|1=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station|1=Sugisaki Station}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan|1=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika|1=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica|1=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu|1=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station|1=Sugisaki Station}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319|1=Hida, Gifu}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, formerly part of {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District|1=Yoshiki District}} in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan|1=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika|1=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica|1=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu|1=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station|1=Sugisaki Station}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.

However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan|1=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika|1=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica|1=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu|1=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station|1=Sugisaki Station}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Takata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Takada shrine, Hida, 2017.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} [[Hida City]] Furukawa Town Tano Kamigakiuchi 2669 | coordinates={{Coord|36.2567194|N|137.1727444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities=[[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Kinpei-sha|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | festivals=April 28 | prefecture={{ill|Hida|WD=Q847319|1=Hida, Gifu}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} }} '''Takata Shrine''' (高田神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Hida City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, formerly part of {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District|1=Yoshiki District}} in [[Furukawa Town]].
It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}. Another theory suggests that {{ill|Kifune Shrine (Hida)|ja|貴船神社 (飛騨市)|1=Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|2=ja|3=貴船神社 (飛騨市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Shrine (Hida City)|qq=}} is the site of the original Takata Shrine.
== History == The exact founding date is unknown. One theory states that it was established in the 6th year of Emperor Bidatsu (577 CE) when the [[Nihamabe|Hihamabe]] clan was stationed in Hida Province, and their {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} enshrined Takamimusubi no Mikoto.
During the [[Kenmu Restoration]], [[Anekōji Ietsuna]] was a11ointed governor of Hida Province in 1334 and showed great reverence for Takata Shrine. He established a residence adjacent to the shrine, which became the political center of Hida. However, after the fall of the {{ill|Anekōji clan|en|Anekōji clan|WD=Q10943126|lt=Anekōji clan|1=Anekōji clan}} to {{ill|Kanamori Nagachika|en|Kanamori Nagachika|WD=Q8190652|lt=Kanamori Nagachika|1=Kanamori Nagachika}}, the shrine's prominence declined.
In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was renamed "Hakusan Shrine" or "Hakusan Gongen Shrine." During the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} (1804–1818), the {{ill|Urabe clan|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|lt=Yoshida clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|qq=}} identified it as the Takata Shrine listed in the Engishiki, and it was renamed accordingly. The formal designation of Takata Shrine as a Shikinaisha was granted in 1870 (Meiji 3).
In 1907 (Meiji 40), the shrine incorporated Suwa Shrine, Tado Shrine, and Kamo Shrine.
== Deities == * Main deity ** [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] * Enshrined Deities ** [[:en:Kukurihime no Kami|Hakusan-hime no Kami]] ** [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] ** {{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|en|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} ** [[Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone no Mikoto]]
== festival == The festival highlights include the Kagura Lion Dance, [[matchlock]] gun firings, and a portable shrine procession.
* Kagura Lion Dance ** A two-person [[shishi-mai]] performed by male and female pairs, featuring six different routines. It is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}.<ref name="kagurajishi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/hidasi/takadajinnjya.html |title=Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> The dance is performed several times during the festival. * Matchlock Gun Firings ** Conducted at key points of the festival to purify the sacred grounds. The matchlocks are said to have been donated during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} by {{ill|Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja|姉小路時光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anekōji Tokimitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Portable Shrine Procession ** A procession including {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Cleyera japonica|1=Cleyera japonica}}, {{ill|Tengu|en|Tengu|WD=Q742360|lt=Tengu|1=Tengu}}, armored warriors, chicken fights, male and female lions, drums, flutes, {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}}, and the mikoshi, comprising several hundred participants.
== Cultural Properties == ;Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Gifu Prefecture * Kagura Lion Dance of Takata Shrine<ref name="kagurajishi" /> ;Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Hida City * Kabuto (helmet) of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pamph-navi.jp/art/view_dynamic/pdfView.php?src=pam10008025 |title=Furukawa Town Cultural Property Guide |format=PDF |publisher=Hida City |accessdate=2013-04-22}}</ref> * Statues of Takata Shrine Guardians<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" /> ;Designated Natural Monuments of Hida City * Five Great Cedars of Takata Shrine<ref name="furukawacho_bunkazai" />
== Access == * Approximately2 km from {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}} {{ill|Sugisaki Station|en|Sugisaki Station|WD=Q7635122|lt=Sugisaki Station|1=Sugisaki Station}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
